data.title: Microservices
data.description: Amazon utilizes a microservices architecture to separate functionalities into independently deployable services.
data.technical_spec: Decoupled services with API communication, typically running on AWS.

data.title: High Scalability
data.description: The infrastructure is built to handle massive traffic and processing needs.
data.technical_spec: Elastic Load Balancing, Autoscaling Groups, and extensive CDN use.

data.title: Distributed Systems
data.description: Amazon's system is globally distributed to ensure site availability across various regions.

data.title: Data-Driven Architecture
data.description: Extensive use of data analytics and machine learning models to enhance user experience.
data.technical_spec: Real-time data processing with AWS Kinesis, Redshift.

data.title: Containerization
data.description: Docker and Kubernetes are leveraged for consistent development and deployment environments.
data.technical_spec: EKS for Kubernetes management, ECS for container orchestration.
